journal_entry.description: Repurchase and cancellation of 10,000 common shares at $6.00 per share
journal_entry.rationale: Under IFRS, share buyback and cancellation reduces equity. Debit Common Shares for par value ($5.00 × 10,000 = $50,000) to reverse the original issuance at par. The $1.00 premium paid above par ($10,000) is debited to Retained Earnings (or a reserve account) as the cost of reacquiring shares above par reduces accumulated equity. Credit Cash for total consideration paid ($6.00 × 10,000 = $60,000). This treatment follows IAS 32, where treasury shares acquired and cancelled reduce equity components.

output.reason: The company paid $60,000 cash (10,000 shares × $6.00) to repurchase and cancel its own shares. On the credit side: Cash is leaving the business = asset decrease (1 line).

credit_classifier.reason: The company paid $60,000 cash (10,000 shares × $6.00) to repurchase and cancel its own shares. On the credit side: Cash is leaving the business = asset decrease (1 line).

output.reason: Share repurchase and cancellation under IFRS: The company paid $60,000 cash (10,000 shares × $6.00) to buy back and cancel its own shares. Debit side includes: (1) Equity decrease for the par value portion = $50,000 (10,000 shares × $5.00 par), and (2) Equity decrease for the excess paid over par = $10,000 premium. Both debits reduce equity accounts (Common Shares and potentially Retained Earnings or Additional Paid-in Capital). Total: 2 equity decrease lines.

debit_classifier.reason: Share repurchase and cancellation under IFRS: The company paid $60,000 cash (10,000 shares × $6.00) to buy back and cancel its own shares.
